findings.issue: The largest tier computed by find-twins.mjs — "paraphrase" (semantic, low-lexical-overlap pairs) — is entirely dropped by compute-furuq.mjs and never reaches the app. This silently discards the single most famous فرق pair in the Quran.
findings.evidence: js/scripts/find-twins.mjs tier counts: {phrase:3708, exact:348, near:136, paraphrase:10121} — paraphrase is 70.7% of all 14,313 catalogued pairs. compute-furuq.mjs:79 `const ALIGN_TIERS = new Set(["exact", "near", "phrase"]);` skips every paraphrase pair (line 84 `if (!ALIGN_TIERS.has(p.tier)) continue;`). Verified: quran-twins.json contains {a:'6:151',b:'17:31',tier:'paraphrase',cos:0.88} but js/apps/studio/public/furuq.json has zero entries for that pair. The app's own TS type (src/furuq.ts:16, `tier: "exact" | "near" | "phrase"`) structurally excludes paraphrase, so this isn't an oversight but an undocumented product decision with no fallback presentation.

findings.issue: «تقديم وتأخير» (reordering, 860 pairs / 20.5% of the catalog) is detected by a set-intersection test with no positional or multiset check, so it mislabels almost every member.
findings.evidence: compute-furuq.mjs:61-62: `const insSet = new Set(ins.map(o=>o.t)); const reordered = del.some(o=>insSet.has(o.t));` — true whenever ANY single deleted word-string also occurs anywhere among inserted words. Measured on all 860 pairs: only 1 (23:83↔27:68, a genuine single-word position swap: «هذا») is a true permutation (equal del/ins multisets); 855/860 (99.4%) have low overlap between the leftover del- and ins-word sets. Average aligned length for this category is 33.4 words with only 23.2% preserved (avg eqFrac 0.232) — i.e. these are mostly large, mostly-different verses, not reorderings. Concrete false positives sampled: 3:195↔47:12 (a martyrs'-reward verse vs. a disbelievers'-fate verse, sharing only the stock phrase «جنات تجري من تحتها الأنهار»/common words) and 9:90↔48:25 (unrelated topics sharing a few function words) — both labeled «تقديم وتأخير».

findings.issue: «مركّب» (2865 pairs / 68.3%, the largest category shown) is dominated by the loosely-defined "phrase" tier (run ≥ 5 shared lemmas, no length normalization), which matches any two verses sharing a recurring Quranic formula regardless of how unrelated the rest of the verse is.
findings.evidence: 2814/2865 (98%) of مركّب pairs come from the phrase tier (find-twins.mjs:137, `run >= 5`). Across all 3708 phrase-tier pairs in quran-twins.json, the shared run covers a median of only 38% of the shorter verse's word length (computed against quran-kg.db word counts); 1094/3708 (29.5%) have coverage under 30%. Confirmed known bad pair 10:104↔22:5 (identity-of-worship statement vs. embryology description — unrelated topics). Additional sampled false positives: 10:68↔20:6, 10:68↔34:1, 10:68↔42:4 — all triggered solely by the generic dominion-formula «...ما في السماوات وما في الأرض», which recurs 27 times across the Quran independent of context (C(27,2)=351 possible pairs from this formula alone); and 2:129↔39:71, 6:78↔60:4, 5:12↔5:65 (eqFrac 0.00–0.02 — essentially unrelated verses). Average eqFrac for the whole category is 0.295 (only ~30% of words genuinely shared). The app already hides this entire category at render time (js/apps/studio/src/views/Furuq.tsx:88, `data.furuq.filter(f => f.cat !== "مركّب")`) — tacit admission it's unusable, but 68% of the computed catalog (and of the 2.5MB furuq.json payload) is dead weight. Even with مركّب hidden, 1079/6236 ayahs (17.3%) still appear in the app's remaining categories — largely inflated by the same false-positive «تقديم وتأخير» pairs above — so the over-tagging problem the owner already fixed once has re-emerged in a new place.

findings.issue: The twin-catalog tiers (find-twins.mjs) are computed on LEMMA sequences, but the word-diff/classification engine (compute-furuq.mjs) aligns on SURFACE text — a mismatch that can render a genuine shared formula as if nothing were shared at all.
findings.evidence: find-twins.mjs uses lemma_id sequences for jaccard/longestRun (lines 28-36, 70-95); compute-furuq.mjs's diff() (lines 39-53) compares `a[i].t === b[j].t` (surface text_clean). Verified concretely: 2:129 and 39:71 share the lemma run [477(رَسُول),38(مِن),276(تَلَى),23(عَلَى),256(آيَة)] — a well-known "prophetic mission" refrain — but every token differs in surface form (رسولا/رسل، منهم/منكم، يتلو/يتلون، عليهم/عليكم، آياتك/آيات), so the rendered diff shows eqFrac=0.00 (literally zero highlighted commonality), making a linguistically-motivated match look like a random unrelated pair. 3 pairs in furuq.json have eq=0 exactly; the broader 10:68-family pairs show artificially low ~28% overlap for the same reason. This also means «اختلاف صيغة» (same-root/different-form) is structurally undercounted, since morphological variants inside a shared run are never recognized as "same slot, different form" — they just register as del/ins noise feeding مركّب.

findings.issue: Documented paraphrase-tier cosine threshold (0.82) does not match the implemented threshold (0.62); the discrepancy currently has small practical effect only because upstream candidate generation (mutual top-8 nearest neighbor) already restricts to strong matches.
findings.evidence: find-twins.mjs:8 (comment) `paraphrase — meaning-close (Gemini cosine ≥ 0.82)` vs find-twins.mjs:138 (code) `(cos ?? 0) >= 0.62 && jac < 0.2`. Measured: of 10,121 paraphrase pairs actually produced, min cosine = 0.80 (never near 0.62), and only 4 pairs fall in [0.80, 0.82); 10,117/10,121 already clear 0.82. So the code path is dead code in practice, but it's still a real, undocumented, silently-inconsistent threshold that would bite if candidate generation ever changes (e.g. widening k beyond mutual-top-8).

findings.issue: classify()'s substitution detector only recognizes single-token-for-single-token adjacent del→ins pairs, so multi-word (block) substitutions get miscounted and dumped into مركّب instead of إبدال/اختلاف صيغة.
findings.evidence: compute-furuq.mjs:64-70 loops over adjacent op pairs only (`ops[k].op==="del" && ops[k+1].op==="ins"`), incrementing subs once per del→ins boundary rather than once per substituted word. Example: 2:95↔62:7 has del=[ولن,يتمنوه], ins=[ولا,يتمنونه] (two clean, correspondingly-ordered word swaps) but the boundary test only fires once (pairing يتمنوه with ولا — the wrong cross-pair), giving subs=1 while del.length=ins.length=2, so the `del.length===subs` check fails and the pair falls to مركّب instead of إبدال. At least 175/2865 (6.1%) مركّب pairs have del.length===ins.length, a necessary (not sufficient) signature of this class of missed clean substitution.

how_it_works: Pipeline: js/scripts/find-twins.mjs builds, per ayah, a lemma-id sequence from `word`/`lemma`/`ayah` tables in quran-kg.db, then finds twin CANDIDATES two ways — (a) an inverted index over lemma 4-grams (lexical), (b) mutual top-8 nearest-neighbor embedding cosine from quran-neighbors.bin (semantic) — and unions them. Each candidate pair is assigned exactly one of four tiers by a priority cascade: exact (surface-identical text) > near (lemma-4-gram Jaccard ≥ 0.45) > phrase (longest common lemma run ≥ 5, no length normalization) > paraphrase (mutual-nearest cosine ≥ 0.62-in-code/0.82-in-comment, with Jaccard < 0.2). Output: quran-twins.json, 14,313 pairs. js/scripts/compute-furuq.mjs then reads that catalog plus per-word surface text + root from quran-kg.db, but only processes tiers exact/near/phrase (`ALIGN_TIERS`, silently dropping the 10,121-pair paraphrase tier). For each kept pair it runs a word-level LCS diff on SURFACE text (not the lemma sequence the tier decision was based on), then classify()s the resulting ops into one of six categories via hand-rolled heuristics: تطابق (no diff), اختلاف صيغة/إبدال (all del/ins tokens pair up as adjacent del→ins with same/different root), زيادة/نقص (one side has zero unique tokens = pure LCS containment), تقديم وتأخير (any deleted word-string reappears anywhere among inserted words — a bare set-intersection test), else مركّب (catch-all). Output: findings/FURUQ.md (human summary) and js/apps/studio/public/furuq.json (app data, 4192 pairs, 2.5MB). The React view (js/apps/studio/src/views/Furuq.tsx) fetches furuq.json via src/furuq.ts, then itself filters OUT every مركّب pair before rendering (line 88) and groups تطابق pairs into \"family\" cards; the other four categories render as two-verse word-aligned comparison cards with a category chip, filterable/searchable by the reader.

proposals.title: Rebuild the alignment backbone on lemma-level LCS, tier by length-normalized coverage
proposals.rationale: Replace the three-way jaccard/run/surface-diff split with ONE metric: run the word LCS at the lemma level (bridging inflectional variants like يتلو/يتلون so they count as "same slot, different form" — feeding اختلاف صيغة correctly instead of vanishing as noise), then compute coverage = matched-lemma-count / min(lenA, lenB). Tier purely by this length-normalized number (e.g. near ≥0.75, phrase 0.45–0.75, drop below 0.45 as "not a twin"). This directly fixes findings #3 and #4 at the source (the 27-times-recurring dominion formula and similar refrains stop qualifying once judged against verse length, not absolute run length) instead of patching them downstream by hiding مركّب in the UI.

proposals.title: Replace the reordering test with a genuine positional/multiset check
proposals.rationale: After removing eq-spans and substitution-blocks (see below), only classify the remainder as «تقديم وتأخير» if the leftover del-token multiset and ins-token multiset are near-identical (e.g. Jaccard of multisets ≥ 0.8) AND the leftover is a small fraction (e.g. ≤30%) of the aligned verse — i.e. "the same words, just moved," not "any word repeats somewhere." On current data this shrinks the category from 860 to roughly the ~1 true instance found (23:83↔27:68) plus whatever few others genuinely qualify — which is the honest number; a rare, high-confidence category beats a common, wrong one.

proposals.title: Fix block-substitution detection so multi-word swaps land in إبدال/اختلاف صيغة, not مركّب
proposals.rationale: Group consecutive del-runs and consecutive ins-runs as ONE substitution unit (compare del-block ↔ ins-block as a whole, check root overlap per aligned position) instead of pairing only the single boundary token. Recovers cases like 2:95↔62:7 (ولن يتمنوه → ولا يتمنونه) that are currently miscounted and dumped into مركّب; helps shrink مركّب's remaining size after the coverage-gate fix above.

proposals.title: Give the paraphrase tier its own honest presentation instead of dropping it
proposals.rationale: Don't force semantic-only pairs (jaccard<0.2 by definition) through the same word-diff categorizer. Ship them as a separate, clearly-labeled "متشابهات المعنى" section: two verses side by side + the cosine score, no forced category. Best-effort: still attempt the lemma-LCS alignment first (e.g. 6:151/17:31 share the root إملاق) — if it clears the coverage gate, promote it into a normal categorized card; otherwise fall back to plain side-by-side. Also resolve the 0.62-vs-0.82 code/comment mismatch by picking one number (recommend ~0.84-0.85, since observed min is already 0.80 and p25 is 0.88 — negligible pair loss, removes the inconsistency, and is defensible given cosine is the only corroborating signal for this tier.

proposals.title: Build a validation harness before shipping the redesign
proposals.rationale: The owner's stated history (v1 over-tagged ~50% of the Quran) means threshold tweaks alone aren't trustworthy without a repeatable check. Concretely: (1) a gold set of ~30-50 classically-cited فروق pairs with expected category, checked for both presence and correct category on every rebuild; (2) an adversarial "formula" test — take the top ~20 highest-frequency ≥5-word lemma runs (recurring refrains) and assert none of their pairwise combinations clear the twin-coverage gate; (3) a build-time printed report of coverage-ratio histograms per tier and category-size ratios, with a sanity bound that مركّب+تقديم-وتأخير together stay a minority of total pairs (currently 89%, inverted from what a well-defined pipeline should produce) so a future regression is caught immediately instead of rediscovered by manual audit months later.

section: فروق التنزيل (near-identical verse pairs pipeline: find-twins.mjs → compute-furuq.mjs → furuq.json → Furuq.tsx)
verdict: The pipeline's well-defined categories are genuinely good: تطابق, اختلاف صيغة, إبدال and زيادة/نقص together (467 pairs, 11.1% of the catalog) sampled as linguistically sound, recognizable «فروق»-style pairs with 63-81% word overlap on average — this part deserves confidence. But the two categories that dominate the actual output — «تقديم وتأخير» (20.5%) and «مركّب» (68.3%), 89% of everything computed — are built on tests that don't test what their names promise: the "reordering" detector is a bare set-intersection that misfires on 855/860 pairs, and "composite" is mostly an artifact of an un-normalized phrase-tier threshold that treats any shared 5-word Quranic refrain (some recur 27+ times) as proof of a "twin" regardless of how unrelated the rest of two 30-40-word verses are. The team already knows مركّب is unusable — it's hard-filtered out of the UI entirely (Furuq.tsx:88) — but that patch discards 68% of computed work rather than fixing generation, and doesn't stop تقديم-وتأخير's false positives from still surfacing to readers (17.3% of all ayahs still appear in the shown categories, largely due to this). Separately, the tier the app never shows at all — paraphrase, 70.7% of the raw catalog — contains the single most famous فرق pair in the Quran (6:151↔17:31), silently dropped by a hardcoded tier allowlist with no documented rationale beyond a code comment. None of this requires new data or infrastructure — quran-kg.db already has everything needed (lemma, root, morphology) — it requires replacing three ad hoc, inconsistently-scaled thresholds (absolute run length, raw jaccard, a doc/code-mismatched cosine) with one length-normalized, lemma-aware alignment metric that both tiers and categorizes, plus an honest fallback presentation for paraphrase, plus a gold-set/adversarial regression test so the next threshold tweak doesn't quietly reintroduce the "half the Quran is متشابه" problem in a new form.
